tracks.title: Neon Pulse
tracks.loreNote: The explosive album opener. Fast-paced and driven by bright, arpeggiated synths, setting the stage for a vibrant night out in the city.

tracks.title: Static on the Wire
tracks.loreNote: A catchy, danceable lead single featuring detached vocals over a driving drum machine and shimmering analog synthesizers.

tracks.title: Concrete Angels
tracks.loreNote: A massive, triumphant stadium-ready rock anthem leaning heavily into Tyler's 'Human Metronome' timing and explosive 80s gated reverb snare.

tracks.title: The Overpass
tracks.loreNote: A driving, altitude-heavy anthem built entirely around Evan Wright’s prominent, melodic bass guitar.

tracks.title: The Horizon Line
tracks.loreNote: The sweeping, hopeful closer to Side A. Features a rare sibling harmony duet that builds into a massive crescendo of synth brass before abruptly ending at twilight.

tracks.title: Midnight Drivers
tracks.loreNote: The title track and the anchor of Side B. A moody, pulsing Darkwave track about finding sanctuary in a moving car at night.

tracks.title: Shatterproof
tracks.loreNote: A cold, rhythmic track heavily influenced by European industrial synth-pop, featuring a detached, robotic vocal delivery from Ryan.

tracks.title: Terminal Velvet
tracks.loreNote: Engineered utilizing The Cosmic Tidal Lock Sound™. A brooding, zero-gravity space-rock ballad showcasing Cassidy's crystal clear vocals and lush spatial reverb.

tracks.title: Digital Ghosts
tracks.loreNote: An ethereal, haunting duet with heavy spatial reverb. The rhythm section is reduced to a sparse, echoing click track, leaving plenty of empty space in the mix.

tracks.title: Fade to Dawn
tracks.loreNote: The epic 1987 album closer. Features a massive 50-second instrumental progressive build leading into a stadium-shaking guitar solo, transitioning the Darkwave tension into pure cathartic rock.

tracks.title: Midnight Drivers (Basement Demo)
tracks.loreNote: A raw, lo-fi 1986 cassette tape recording of Cassidy and Ryan writing the title track on an upright piano in their parents' basement.

tracks.title: Neon Pulse (12" Extended Club Mix)
tracks.loreNote: A hypnotic, 5-minute DJ remix engineered for underground dance clubs, featuring a heavy four-on-the-floor beat and extended instrumental synthesizer breaks.

tracks.title: Static on the Wire (Instrumental B-Side)
tracks.loreNote: The original B-Side to the 45 RPM single. A vocal-free version showcasing the band's raw synthesizer programming and studio engineering.

tracks.title: The Engine Room (Studio Jam)
tracks.loreNote: A gritty, unpolished studio outtake of Evan and Tyler laying down a heavy drum and bass jam in a closed session. Pure rhythm section muscle.
